title: Elementary storage occupations n.e.c.
description: All construction trades helpers not listed separately. Maintain highways, municipal and rural roads, airport runways, and rights-of-way. Duties include patching broken or eroded pavement and repairing guard rails, highway markers, and snow fences. May also mow or clear brush from along road, or plow snow from roadway. Lay, repair, and maintain track for standard or narrow-gauge railroad equipment used in regular railroad service or in plant yards, quarries, sand and gravel pits, and mines. Includes ballast cleaning machine operators and railroad bed tamping machine operators. (Based on 11 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Maintain highways, municipal and rural roads, airport runways, and rights-of-way, including patching broken or eroded pavement.
Drive trucks to transport crews and equipment to work sites and operate equipment for clearing, mopping, and maintaining roads.
Inspect, clean, and repair drainage systems, bridges, tunnels, and other structures; remove litter and debris from roadways.
Lay, repair, and maintain track for standard or narrow-gauge railroad equipment used in regular railroad service or in plant yards.
Chip and clean rust spots on decks, and clean and repair hulls and superstructures of vessels, using power tools and hand tools.
Perform preventative maintenance on vehicles and heavy equipment, including lubricating machinery and servicing motors.
Oversee operation of vessels used for carrying passengers, motor vehicles, or goods across rivers, harbors, lakes, and coastal waters.
Provide training to crew members in safety procedures, equipment operation, and assist engineers with machinery repairs.
Perform roadside landscaping work, including clearing weeds and brush, and planting or trimming trees.
Direct crew members in daily activities and report any operational issues or observed navigational hazards to authorities.
Plan work schedules and assign duties to maintain adequate staff for effective performance of activities.
Record operating data such as products and quantities pumped, gauge readings, and operating times, manually or using computers.
Ensure compliance with safety procedures and maintain government-issued certifications as required.
Inspect equipment for wear and for conformance to specifications.
Inspect job sites to determine the extent of maintenance or repairs needed.
Move freight, stock, or other materials to and from storage or production areas using trucks, tractors, or other equipment.
Read work orders or receive oral instructions to determine work assignments or material or equipment needs.
Sort materials, such as metals, glass, wood, paper or plastics, into appropriate containers for recycling.
Collect and sort recyclable construction materials, such as concrete, drywall, plastics, or wood, into containers.
Operate forklifts, pallet jacks, or front-end loaders to load bales, bundles, or other heavy items onto trucks.
Clean recycling yard by sweeping, raking, or moving barrels and bins.
Clean, inspect, or lubricate recyclable collection equipment or perform routine maintenance on recycling equipment.
Maintain equipment storage areas to ensure that inventory is protected.
